seoTitle: How to Make Flowcharts in Microsoft Loop With Mermaid
metaDescription: Build live flowcharts in Microsoft Loop using Mermaid.js syntax, from setting orientation to connecting nodes with labeled arrows.
intro: Rather than exporting static flowchart images from a separate design tool, you can write Mermaid.js code directly inside a Microsoft Loop page and have it render as a live, editable flowchart. This guide covers setting up the diagram block, defining node shapes, and connecting them into a working process map.

steps.heading: Open the diagram code block
steps.body: Move your cursor to a new line in your Loop page and type the /mermaid slash command to deploy the diagram script container.

steps.heading: Set your flowchart orientation
steps.body: Type flowchart TD for a top-down layout or flowchart LR for left-to-right at the top of your code block to define the diagram's direction.

steps.heading: Define your nodes
steps.body: Give each step a short identifier and use brackets or curly braces to style the text as rectangles, rounded blocks, or decision diamonds.

steps.heading: Connect the nodes with arrows
steps.body: Use arrow shorthand and vertical pipes to link your nodes together, adding labels for conditional paths where needed.

steps.heading: Preview and share the flowchart
steps.body: Toggle between code view and split-screen live preview to check your layout, then let Loop auto-sync the rendered diagram across your shared workspace.

faq.question: Do I need special software to build flowcharts this way?
faq.answer: No, Mermaid diagrams render natively inside Loop's editor, so you don't need a separate drafting tool.

faq.question: Can I make decision diamonds in a Mermaid flowchart?
faq.answer: Yes, using curly brace syntax around a node's text renders it as a diamond shape suited for decision points.

faq.question: What's the difference between flowchart TD and flowchart LR?
faq.answer: TD lays the flowchart out top-down, while LR arranges it left-to-right — the choice depends on which orientation fits your process better.

faq.question: Will my flowchart update automatically for teammates?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's rendered live from code on a shared page, Loop syncs updates to the diagram across your team's shared channels.
